pr.title: fix: the custom strcpy(char *dst, char *src) and str... in...
pr.author: orbisai0security

summary: Unmerged automated security-fix PR that changed only the extern Dolphin charPipeline dolphinString header prototypes for Strcat and Strcpy by adding a u32 dst_size parameter. The PR body framed this as a critical bounds-checking fix, but the diff shows only declaration changes and no implementation or callsite updates, so evidence for an actual safe/matching fix is weak.
what_changed: Changed the Strcat declaration from `u8 Strcat(char *str1, char *str2, char *dst);` to `u8 Strcat(char *str1, char *str2, char *dst, u32 dst_size);`.
Changed the Strcpy declaration from `void Strcpy(char *dst, char *src);` to `void Strcpy(char *dst, char *src, u32 dst_size);`.
No other files were changed in the slice: 1 file, 2 added lines, 2 deleted lines.
The PR was closed with `merged_at` null and has no issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the provided dump.
smart_moves: The reusable safety idea is to make destination-buffer capacity explicit when designing copy/concatenate APIs.
The PR targeted the narrow header location identified in its scanner report rather than making broad unrelated edits.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation work, changing an external SDK/header ABI is risky unless the original binary/API evidence supports the new signature. Header-only prototype changes can desynchronize declarations from existing implementations, symbols, and callsites.
A real bounds-checking conversion needs implementation changes and callsite migration, not just adding a `dst_size` parameter to declarations.
Automated security-scanner PRs against historical/decomp code should be treated cautiously: preserving original SDK behavior and matching constraints may matter more than modernizing APIs.
When evidence is limited to a diff of prototypes, do not infer that runtime bounds checks were added.
naming_conventions: Existing Dolphin-style function names in this header use capitalized names: `Strcat`, `Strcpy`, `Strcmp`, `Strlen`.
The added size parameter was named `dst_size`, using lower_snake_case, but there is no evidence this naming matches original Dolphin SDK conventions.
Types in this header use project/Dolphin typedefs such as `u8`, `s8`, and `u32`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly, matching strategy, or symbol-matching evidence appears in the PR dump.
Future matching work should verify any proposed signature change against implementation assembly and all callsites before accepting it.
review_feedback: No review comments, issue comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice.
The PR was closed unmerged, but the dump does not include maintainer rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find implementations and callsites of `Strcat` and `Strcpy` before considering any signature or bounds-checking change.
Check whether `dolphinString.h` is intended to mirror an original Dolphin SDK header and whether these signatures must remain ABI-compatible.
Investigate why PR #2474 was closed unmerged if external discussion exists outside the provided dump.
